title: Consultation on guidelines for Canadian drinking water quality: Radiological Parameters - Canada.ca
content:
## Consultation on guidelines for Canadian drinking water quality: Radiological Parameters

## Current status: Closed
Input received from October 11, 2024 to January 10, 2025.
This guideline technical document outlines the available information on radionuclides with the intent of updating the guidelines for radionuclides in drinking water.
The purpose of this consultation is to solicit comments on the proposed guidelines for Radiological Parameters, on the approach used for their development, and on the potential impacts of implementing them.
We're looking for comments from regulatory authorities, decision makers and the public on the draft document.

## Join in: how to participate
Send an email to water-eau@hc-sc.gc.ca with your ideas or comments to make yourself heard.
Send a letter with your ideas and input to the address in contact information below.

## Who is the focus of this consultation
We will engage with:
- federal, provincial and territorial regulatory and health protection agencies
- industry stakeholders
- academic communities
- non-governmental organizations

## Key questions for discussion
Your ideas and inputs are sought around 2 topics:
- the proposed approach
- the potential impacts of implementation
We will use the information we gather from this consultation to revise the document as needed.
- Guidelines for Canadian drinking water quality: Radiological parameters
Water and Air Quality Bureau Health Canada 269 Laurier Ave West Address Locator 4903B Ottawa ON K1A 0K9 Email: water-eau@hc-sc.gc.ca
